The operational profit decrease of 9% was primarily attributed to lower sales volumes (14%) as well as higher selling, general and administrative expenses (3%), including higher costs associated with Carrier's transition to an independent, publicly traded company. These decreases were partially offset by favorable material productivity and cost containment initiatives (7%).
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments such as HVAC, particularly residential HVAC, but also experiencing declines in lower-margin segments like transport refrigeration and certain regions due to the pandemic.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 3% for the three months ended September 30, 2020 compared with the same period of the prior year, reflecting organic sales growth of 11% in HVAC, partially offset by lower organic sales of 6% in Refrigeration and 7% in Fire & Security.

The organic sales decrease of 11% reflects lower product (11%) and field service (12%) sales. The decline in product sales was primarily driven by lower volume in North America and EMEA due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: The organic sales increase of 11% was driven by North America residential HVAC (46%) due to strong residential sales, partially offset by declines in North America light commercial HVAC (13%) and Commercial HVAC (7%).
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with some areas showing growth while others face declines or challenges. The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic is evident, contributing to decelerating demand in certain regions and sectors, particularly hospitality and industrial businesses in North America and EMEA, while other areas like commercial fire products in North America and China show relative strength.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 3% for the three months ended September 30, 2020 compared with the same period of the prior year, reflecting organic sales growth of 11% in HVAC, partially offset by lower organic sales of 6% in Refrigeration and 7% in Fire & Security.

The organic sales decrease of 7% reflects lower product (7%) and field service (7%) sales. The decline in product sales was primarily driven by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on hospitality and industrial businesses in North America and EMEA, partially offset by strength in commercial fire products, primarily in North America and China.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: The strength in residential was driven by new housing starts and higher demand for replacement units due to increased usage attributed to a warmer than normal summer and remote work and school activities associated with COVID-19. Commercial HVAC sales declined globally except in China, which saw an increase.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth, primarily driven by the economic slowdowns resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic. This decline is evident across various segments, including commercial and transport refrigeration, with specific impacts on hospitality, industrial, and HVAC sectors. While there are slight offsets in certain regions like North America residential HVAC and commercial fire products in North America and China, the overall trend indicates a negative impact on organic growth.

evidence.text: Organic sales for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 reflects lower sales volumes across all of the segments driven by the economic slowdowns attributed to the COVID-19 pandemic in the first half of the year.

The organic sales decrease of 4% reflects declines in commercial HVAC (10%) and light commercial HVAC (16%) largely driven by the economic slowdowns related to the COVID-19 pandemic, partially offset by increases in North America residential HVAC (6%).

factors.summary: Management is exhibiting cautious behavior due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, as well as restructuring activities.

evidence.text: The full extent of the impact of COVID-19 on our operational and financial performance will depend on future developments, including the duration and spread of the pandemic as well as any worsening or additional outbreaks of the pandemic, and related containment and mitigation actions taken by the U.S., state and local and international governments to prevent disease spread.

The pandemic continues to result in widespread travel restrictions and reduced travel as well as extended or partial shutdowns and other restrictions on the operations of non-essential businesses, including construction and hospitality venues.

We are continuously evaluating our cost structure and have implemented restructuring actions to keep our cost structure competitive. The amounts reflected previously include the impact of restructuring actions on Selling, general and administrative expenses. For additional discussion, see 'Restructuring Costs' and Note 15 – Restructuring Costs to the Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is centered on innovation, sustainability, and operational efficiency, with a focus on market leadership and cost management. However, the details of their strategic direction are not explicitly provided in some sections, indicating a need for further clarification.

evidence.text: The company's long-term strategy is focused on innovation and sustainability, supported by industry-leading brands.

Restructuring actions are a component of our operating margin improvement efforts and relate to existing and recently acquired operations. Charges generally arise from severance related to workforce reductions, facility exit and lease termination costs associated with the consolidation of field and manufacturing operations and costs to exit legacy programs. We continue to closely monitor the economic environment and may undertake further restructuring actions to keep our cost structure aligned with the demand for our products and services and prevailing market conditions.

factors.summary: Near-term risks primarily stem from the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic, including potential rollbacks of reopening measures, economic slowdowns, and disruptions in operations, particularly affecting commercial and transport refrigeration segments. Additionally, cost containment initiatives have been implemented to mitigate some of these challenges.

evidence.text: Near-term risks include ongoing disruptions from the COVID-19 pandemic and economic slowdowns.

Further prolonged closures and restrictions throughout the world or the rollback of reopening measures due to a resurgence of COVID-19 cases and continued decreases in the general level of economic activity may further disrupt our operations and the operations of our suppliers, distributors and customers.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and may be further impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The decrease in Selling, general and administrative expenses in the nine months ended September 30, 2020 was primarily driven by cost containment initiatives implemented to mitigate the impact of COVID-19 on our businesses. Such cost containment actions included furloughs, temporary pay freezes and pay cuts and reductions in discretionary spending across the business.

factors.summary: The company's key sensitivities include the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, economic conditions, and raw material costs, which have affected operational profit and sales volumes. Additionally, foreign exchange movements and their influence on margins and demand are also significant factors.

evidence.text: The operational profit decrease of 9% was primarily attributed to lower sales volumes (14%) as well as higher selling, general and administrative expenses (3%), including higher costs associated with Carrier's transition to an independent, publicly traded company.

The increase in the effective tax rate for the three months ended September 30, 2020 compared with the prior year is primarily due to the absence of a net tax reduction resulting from separation-related activities impacting non-U.S. deferred taxes, partially offset by a tax reduction for the 2020 tax year from the finalization of the U.S. Treasury GILTI HTE regulations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company faces mixed catalysts for improved results. On one hand, the recovery in demand as the COVID-19 pandemic subsides, particularly in the HVAC segment, presents a positive development. On the other hand, while cost containment initiatives and favorable material productivity have contributed to improved results, there are also restructuring costs and ongoing workforce reductions that could impact performance.

evidence.text: A key catalyst is the recovery in demand as the COVID-19 pandemic subsides, particularly in the HVAC segment.
favorable material productivity and cost containment initiatives (7%)

evidence.text: During the nine months ended September 30, 2020, we recorded net pre-tax restructuring charges of $18 million relating to ongoing cost reduction actions initiated in 2020. For actions initiated in 2020, we are targeting to complete the majority of the remaining workforce and facility-related cost reductions in 2021. During the nine months ended September 30, 2020, we had $8 million of cash outflows related to the 2020 actions. As of September 30, 2020, we expect to incur additional restructuring and other charges of $4 million to complete these actions.

factors.summary: The company has engaged in M&A activity, specifically the sale of Beijer shares in September 2020, which contributed to other income and resulted in a pre-tax gain. However, the Fire & Security subsection does not provide specific information on other mergers and acquisitions.

evidence.text: M&A activity includes the sale of Beijer shares, contributing to other income, but no further details on acquisitions or divestitures are provided.

In September 2020, the Company sold 9.25 million B shares of Beijer for SEK290 ($32.38) per share equal to approximately 7.9% of the outstanding B shares in Beijer, through an accelerated equity offering. We received proceeds of approximately $300 million and recognized a pre-tax gain on the sale of $252 million. Following the sale, Beijer, which is listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm, continues to be reported as an equity method investment with Carrier continuing to hold approximately 30% of Beijer's B shares, approximately 22% of Beijer's A shares.
